name: Breath cycle timing
description: Breath-phase durations on the ventilator (inspiratory, expiratory, inspiratory pause times), in seconds.

en.name: Breath cycle timing
en.shortDescription: Breath-phase durations on the ventilator (inspiratory, expiratory, inspiratory pause times), in seconds.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Breath cycle timing covers the durations of the phases of a ventilator breath — inspiratory time, expiratory time, and the inspiratory pause (the no-flow hold at the end of inspiration). These determine the inspiratory:expiratory (I:E) ratio. In source data each is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, set or measured by the ventilator. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. They are reported in seconds (s).

## Included Concepts

The set lists the SNOMED and LOINC concepts for breath-phase durations. All are reported in seconds.

### Inspiratory time

- *4353947 / 250819002 — Inspiratory time (SNOMED)*.
- *3043415 / 33435-9 — Inspiratory breath [Time] --on ventilator (LOINC)*: measured inspiratory time.
- *36304672 / 76334-2 — Inspiratory time setting Ventilator (LOINC)*: set inspiratory time.

### Expiratory time

- *4353622 / 250820008 — Expiratory time (SNOMED)*.

### Inspiratory pause time

- *4353623 / 250821007 — Inspiratory pause time (SNOMED)*.
- *36303637 / 76227-8 — Inspiratory pause time Ventilator (LOINC)*: measured pause time.
- *36303489 / 76226-0 — Inspiratory pause time setting Ventilator (LOINC)*: set pause time.

**Note on units**: reported in **seconds** (`8555 / s — second`, UCUM), the recommended unit.

## Mapping Notes

- Map a recorded breath-phase duration to the matching concept by phase (inspiratory, expiratory, inspiratory pause).
- **Setting vs measured**: for inspiratory time and pause time, map the set value to the corresponding LOINC 'setting' concept and the measured value to the measured concept; use the generic SNOMED concept when the source does not distinguish them.
- **Inspiratory time as a percentage (Ti%)** is a different measurement with a different unit (%) and is not part of this set; the I:E ratio is likewise a separate, dimensionless measure.

fr.name: Durée du cycle respiratoire
fr.shortDescription: Durées des phases du cycle ventilatoire (temps inspiratoire, expiratoire, pause inspiratoire), en secondes.

createdByDetails.affiliation: Univ Rennes, CHU Rennes, INSERM, LTSI-UMR 1099, Rennes, France

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Describe breath cycle timing; add LOINC inspiratory-time (measured 33435-9 + setting 76334-2) and inspiratory-pause-time (measured 76227-8 + setting 76226-0) concepts; register seconds recommended unit. Inspiratory time percent (Ti%, %) kept out (different unit). Resolves to 7.
